steps.title: Recognize the same line
steps.content: The second equation is $4$ times the first: $$4(3x+2y=11) gives 12x+8y=44.$$ So a point only needs to satisfy $3x+2y=11$ to lie on both graphs.

steps.title: Use r as the y-value
steps.content: The answer choices are written in terms of $r$. Let $y=r$ and solve the first equation for $x$: $$3x+2r=11$$ $$3x=11-2r$$ $$x=-2r/3+11/3$$

steps.title: Match the point
steps.content: The point is therefore $(-2r/3+11/3,r)$, which matches B.
